symptom: Customer wants to check if their Purview account has consented to the new Data Governance billing model (new pricing)
rootCause: New pricing model requires explicit consent; without checking, unclear if account is on new or old billing
solution: Use Kusto query on babylon.eastus2.kusto.windows.net / babylonMdsLogs: 1) Query AccountInfo with tenantId to get AccountId, 2) Query AccountInfo with AccountId, filter ReconciledStatus=='Active' and MspConsent contains 'consentState', parse consentState and consentedAt. No result = no consent action taken.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Apps - Data Governance/New Pricing Model: Microsoft Purview Data Governance Enterprise Solution

symptom: User navigates to new Data Catalog > Roles and permissions and sees 'You need permission to access this' error after upgrading from classic Azure Purview to Unified Purview Portal
rootCause: When upgrading from classic Azure Purview, only the AAD identity of the original Purview Account creator is placed in the Data Governance Admin role. Other users are not automatically granted access.
solution: Three options: 1) Use self-service option to add users to Data Governance role (docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/data-catalog-permissions#how-to-assign-tenant-level-role-groups), 2) Identify account creator via Azure Portal > Purview account > Properties > Created By field, contact them to add users, 3) If creator unavailable, create ICM/AVA to 'Purview Data Governance > Data Catalog > Management' queue requesting to add user (AAD username + Object ID) to Data Governance Admin role. If account was created by SPN, the same SPN can add users via API.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Apps - Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides/Curation (Biz Domains, Data Products & Permissions)/Reprovision a user to Data Governance admin role

symptom: Customer observes unexpected charges under 'Data Management Basic Data Governance Processing Unit (DGPU)' despite not running any Purview scans
rootCause: DGPUs are automatically spun up for Data Estate Health (DEH) operations, which perform ongoing monitoring and maintenance of data governance in Purview Unified Catalog, causing continuous charges even without explicit scanning.
solution: Review the Data Health Billing Awareness guidance to understand DEH charges and how to stop them. DEH operations are separate from user-initiated scans.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Apps - Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides/Data Estate Health/Clarification on Data Management Basic Data Governance Processing Unit (DGPU) Charges Due to Data Estate Health (DEH)

symptom: When trying to add data assets to a data product, request fails with status code 400 and error message: The name contains invalid character(s). Try another name.
rootCause: Table schema contains forbidden characters such as newline, tab, only whitespace, or other non-printable ASCII control characters in column names.
solution: Edit the schema in Data Map to remove the forbidden/non-printable characters from column names.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Apps - Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides/Data Products/Prerequisites and handling errors in Data Product

symptom: Customer needs to use Fabric Lakehouse for Data Quality scans with Private Purview / Purview Data Map and Fabric OneLake, but access is blocked without allow-listing
rootCause: Private Purview + Fabric OneLake integration for DQ scans requires explicit tenant allow-listing by PG during Private Preview.
solution: Contact PM (Sri Kutuva srik@microsoft.com or Shafiq Mannan shafiqul.mannan@microsoft.com) with: Tenant ID, Organization Name, Account Name, Account ID, Catalog ID, Region. Use Kusto query on babylon.eastus2.kusto.windows.net/babylonMdsLogs.AccountInfo to look up account details. Verify ReconciledStatus=Active and AccountTier=EnterpriseTier.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Apps - Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides/Data Quality/Allow-List for Private Purview + Fabric OneLake Cx

symptom: Data Quality scan fails with error: Profiling job failed / DataQualityInternalError / Internal service error occurred. Kusto logs show scala.MatchError: NullType
rootCause: An automatically selected column from a previous profile no longer exists in the customer dataset (column was renamed or deleted), causing a NullType match error in the scan engine.
solution: Remove the stale/missing column from the DQ scan settings and re-run the scan. Use Kusto query on babylon.eastus2.kusto.windows.net/babylonMdsLogs.DataQualityLogEvent with CorrelationId to confirm the NullType error.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Apps - Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides/Data Quality/DQ Scan Failure : DataQualityInternalError

symptom: Purview Data Quality and Data Profile scans fail after customer upgraded Delta Lake beyond version 2.2. Scan/Profile options may also appear disabled for new data assets.
rootCause: Purview DQ only supports Delta Lake up to version 2.2. Upgrading to Delta Lake 2.4 or later causes scan incompatibility and failures.
solution: Customer must downgrade to Delta Lake 2.2.0 for DQ scans and data profiles until Purview DQ adds support for newer Delta Lake versions. No ETA for new version support at time of writing.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Apps - Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides/Data Quality/DQ and Profile scan fail

symptom: DQ scan fails with error: Max Retry Count Reached. Ending Workflow. Current Task HandleError. Backend logs show: The given key (region name) was not present in the dictionary.
rootCause: Customer data source is in a region not supported for Data Quality scans. Both the Purview account and data source must be in one of the supported regions.
solution: Verify both Purview account and data source are in supported DQ regions. See supported regions: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/data-catalog-regions. Customer may need to relocate data source to a supported region.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Apps - Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides/Data Quality/DQ scan failure - The given key 'xxxxx' was not present in the dictionary

symptom: DQ scan fails with Max Retry Count Reached and Kusto logs show The given key northcentralus was not present in the dictionary
rootCause: Data source is in a region not currently supported for Data Quality scans
solution: Ensure both Purview account and data source are in supported DQ regions. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/data-catalog-regions
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Apps - Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides/Data Quality/DQ scan failure - The given key xxxxx was not present in the dictionary

symptom: Data Quality scans on Fabric Lakehouse tables fail with Not found (Asset path not found) or DQ Internal Error when scanning Fabric Data Map assets
rootCause: Fabric Lakehouse DQ support in private preview. Data Map Scan indexed child artifact IDs with no info, semantic model scanning inferred incorrect table paths. Tenant needs 3-level allowlisting.
solution: Allowlist via CSAM (CSS cannot do this) at 3 levels: metadata harvesting, Fabric traffic control, Purview DQ. After: publish tables delta/parquet, scan with SP credential, verify Lakehouse Table catalog item, add to Data Product, create DQ Connection with Purview MSI.

symptom: Data profiling of Fabric or Power BI fails with error: Unable to submit job to PFS with exception: Unexpected character encountered while parsing value
rootCause: Wrong permission on PBI Workspace for Purview MSI. Must have exactly Contributor role (not any other role, not even higher).
solution: 1) Give Purview MSI Contributor access (exactly Contributor) to PBI workspace. 2) Re-scan Fabric in Data Map. 3) Remove assets from data product. 4) After Data Map scan finishes, add assets again.

rootCause: User not part of the Data Governance role group. Being Purview Administrator alone is insufficient for Data Governance page access.
solution: Add user to Data Governance role group via Purview settings. Data Governance role group grants Data Governance Administrator. Ref: learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/governance-roles-permissions

rootCause: Invalid characters in Fabric workspace name (e.g. [Biz][D]) cause URL parsing failure. Integration does not support brackets.
solution: Remove unsupported characters (brackets etc.) from Fabric workspace name and retry. Note: Issue already fixed by PG.

rootCause: Missing role assignments (Owner, Data Curator, Data Source Admin, Reader, Collection Admin). After Aug 2021, access managed via collections. Private VNET requires client on same VNET.

rootCause: Governance domain must be unpublished and all business concepts (data products, assets with DQ data) within it must be deleted first.
solution: 1) Unpublish governance domain. 2) Delete data quality data from assets. 3) Remove assets from data products. 4) Delete data products. 5) Delete all business concepts. 6) Delete button becomes available.

solution: Cannot restore. Advise DR/backup setup: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/disaster-recovery

rootCause: Sovereign clouds require dSTS-Federated authentication and specific cluster URLs. Standard public cloud Kusto connection does not work.
solution: 1) Join security group 'Redmond\Mooncake DB PoD access' via IdWeb. 2) In Kusto Explorer, add connection with cluster URL: https://purviewadxcn3.chinanorth3.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn, databases: BabylonMdsLogs, DataScanLogs. 3) Set Client Security to 'dSTS-Federated'. 4) Mooncake ASC URL: https://azuresupportcenter.chinacloudapi.cn/resourceexplorerv2

rootCause: Fairfax (Gov) cloud requires government clearance (SAVM membership) and JIT access via ICM ticket. Cannot use standard dev machine access.
solution: 1) Get SAVM membership via https://cloudmfa-support.azurewebsites.net/SawSupportServices/SAVM (select datacenter region matching geo). 2) Access AVD via https://aka.ms/savmTDC. 3) Install Kusto Desktop on jump box. 4) Add Fairfax cluster: https://purviewadxbn.usgovvirginia.kusto.usgovcloudapi.net. For ADF logs: create JIT ICM ticket, PG provides jump box info.

symptom: AD RMS management console Health and Troubleshooting reports unavailable; link in GUI tries to access non-existent location for Report Viewer tool
rootCause: Missing .NET Framework 3.5 Features and Report Viewer Redistributable 2008 SP1 on the AD RMS server
solution: 1) Add .NET Framework 3.5 Features via Server Manager Add Roles and Features Wizard. 2) Download and install Report Viewer Redistributable 2008 SP1 from Microsoft (KB971119 / download ID 3203). 3) Close and reopen the AD RMS MMC to access reports.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/How To: AD RMS/How To: Add AD RMS Reports Functionality

symptom: AD RMS group membership changes not reflected; users cannot access protected content despite being added to authorized groups; permission changes take up to 24 hours to take effect
rootCause: AD RMS caches Active Directory directory services lookups in the Directory Services database for 12+ hours; group membership changes may take up to 24 hours to update in the AD RMS cache
solution: Clear AD RMS directory services cache by running DELETE statements on PrincipalAliases, GroupAliases, GroupMembership, PrincipalIdentifiers, PrincipalMembership, and GroupIdentifiers tables in DRMS_DirectoryServices database, then perform IIS reset (iisreset) on ALL cluster members. Optionally disable caching entirely by setting all DirectoryServices*Cache* policies to 0 in DRMS_ClusterPolicies table.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/How To: AD RMS/How To: DS lookup caching

symptom: Custom classification tags not applied after running scan - changes to classification rules made while scan is already running are not reflected
rootCause: Classification settings are retrieved when scan starts; changes to custom classification rules during a running scan do not take effect until a new scan is started
solution: Stop the current scan, update the classification rule, then start a new scan with the updated rules. Verify the scan ruleset includes the custom classification rule.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Classification and sensitivity labels/Applying Custom classification rules to successful scans/Custom classification not showing correct tags

symptom: Classification missing on single asset (non-resource set) - system classification not applied even though data appears to meet requirements
rootCause: Column has fewer than 8 distinct values, which is the minimum required for system classification rules to be applied
solution: Ensure column has at least 8 distinct values. Check classification best practices at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/purview/concept-best-practices-classification. For scope scans, verify target asset is included by querying DataScanAgentLinuxEvent for 'Included Paths'. For SHIR scans use TraceGatewayLocalEventLog instead of DataScanAgentLinuxEvent.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Classification and sensitivity labels/Classification is missing or incorrect in single asset (non-resource set asset)

symptom: Deleted asset from data source still appears in Purview catalog with classifications after scan completes
rootCause: Incremental scan only processes new/modified assets; deleted assets are not removed until a full scan is triggered
solution: Ask customer to run a full scan (not incremental). Verify scan type by checking OpInfo/EntityInfo for IsFullScan field. If full scan was run and asset still shows: check if OpInfo was emitted for the asset - if not, file IcM with Offline/Data Map team; if OpInfo still emitted, file IcM with DataScan team.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Classification and sensitivity labels/Deleting or removing a Classification/Deleted classification still available on an asset

symptom: MCE classification (e.g., US Driver's License) not applied or incorrectly classified on data
rootCause: MCE rules require specific contextual keywords AND proper formatting. For example, US DL needs keywords from Keyword_us_drivers_license_abbreviations (e.g., DL), Keyword_us_drivers_license (e.g., Drivers License in column name), AND Keyword_[state_name]_drivers_license_name (e.g., NY), plus the number must match the state-specific format (e.g., NY: 427 676 936 not 427676936)
solution: Check MCE entity definitions (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/compliance/sensitive-information-type-entity-definitions) to verify data meets all conditions: contextual keywords, column name patterns, and number formatting per state/country. Supported classifications: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/purview/supported-classifications
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Classification and sensitivity labels/Incorrect classification insights report

symptom: First Name, Last Name or other classification tags missing on columns in CSV resource set files after successful Purview scan
rootCause: For resource set files, Purview only samples ~1% of files (e.g., 10 out of 1000) and reads 1MB per file. If sampled files have bad data (e.g., only 1 distinct value in the column), classification thresholds are not met. This is by design.
solution: 1) Check OpInfo/EntityInfo using DataScanAgentEvent to see which files were sampled and column distinct values. 2) If system classification thresholds can't be met, advise customer to create custom classification rules with lower distinct value thresholds. 3) For resource sets, customer should ensure sampled files contain representative data. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/purview/concept-resource-sets#how-azure-purview-scans-resource-sets
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Classification and sensitivity labels/Missing or incorrectly classified assets/Missing classification for columns in CSV files

symptom: Classification tags missing on asset despite data appearing to match classification rules (threshold issue)
rootCause: Classification thresholds not met. For structured data: MinSamples=8 distinct values required, MinCount=2 distinct matching samples, delimited files must have >=3 columns for cut Module. Confidence threshold: <0.6 won't appear in OpInfo, 0.6-0.8 appears in OpInfo but NOT in UI, >0.8 appears in UI.
solution: 1) Check OpInfo/EntityInfo for Tags and distinctCount values. 2) If Tags field missing → first three thresholds not met. 3) If confidence <0.8 → file IcM with DataScan team. 4) If confidence >0.8 but tag still missing in UI → file IcM with Offline Tier team. Always provide: RunID, sample data (first 32 rows), asset/file name, column name, expected classification tag.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Classification and sensitivity labels/Missing or incorrectly classified assets/Missing classification of assets due to low threshold values

symptom: Classification tags missing from scanned asset; OpInfo not emitted for the asset in DataScanAgentLinuxEvent logs
rootCause: Purview scan cannot access data source folders due to ACL/firewall restrictions, causing 403 Forbidden errors in the find module
solution: Query DataScanAgentLinuxEvent for exceptions in find module (Source == 'find'). If 403 errors found, customer must configure ACL and firewall rules in data source to allow Purview access. If issue persists, file ICM with DataScan providing RunID, asset name, and exception details.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Classification and sensitivity labels/Missing or incorrectly classified assets/Missing classifications on an asset

symptom: Sensitivity label not auto-applied to asset; customer is using custom Sensitive Information Types (SITs) in auto-labeling rules for schematized data
rootCause: Custom SITs created in Microsoft Purview Information Protection are NOT supported for auto-labeling schematized data assets in Purview Data Map. Only system-created SITs work with auto-labeling.
solution: Advise customer to use system-created Sensitive Information Types instead of custom ones for auto-labeling. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/sensitivity-labels-frequently-asked-questions
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Classification and sensitivity labels/Missing or incorrectly labeled assets/Label is missing on an asset
Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Glossary/Known Issues/Service Principal as People Contact unsupported
solution: Do not use service principal as people contact. If customer needs this, consult on their scenario and contact PG team to log a backlog item
symptom: Importing/exporting service principal as people contact in glossary terms is unsupported; only manual assignment via UI works
rootCause: Service principal is not a formally supported people contact type in Microsoft Purview glossary

symptom: Oracle scan classification fails with ORA-00942 table or view does not exist when scanning tables owned by a different schema user than the credential user
rootCause: Purview Oracle connector does not support cross-schema classification. User A in credential cannot classify tables under User B schema. The ADF Oracle driver read function fails when accessing tables across schemas.
solution: Ensure username in scan credential matches the schema owner of the tables requiring classification. Workaround: make username and schema name the same when defining user in the credential section.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Classification and sensitivity labels/Oracle - Cross schema classification failure

symptom: Manual edits to asset (description, glossary term, classification) in Purview Governance Portal are overwritten or lost after subsequent scan runs
rootCause: By design, manual changes made in Edit Asset screen cannot coexist with subsequent scan updates. A scan re-ingests metadata and may overwrite manual edits. Enhancement to preserve manual description/glossary/classification during scan is planned.
solution: Known limitation. For bulk edit issues see sub-pages. Enhancement planned to allow manual description, glossary term, and classification edits to persist across scan runs.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map Assets/Editing Assets

symptom: Cannot export or download glossary terms as CSV files from Purview portal; export button appears stuck or download does not start
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: (1) Browser blocks automatic downloads from Purview website, (2) Export API timeout when too many terms selected, (3) Export API call failure
solution: (1) Check browser site settings: set Automatic downloads to Ask or Allow. (2) Try exporting smaller subset of terms. (3) Verify Exporting button changes state and check F12 network for 200 status. (4) If all fail, get x-ms-client-request-id from error/F12, search Kusto OnlineTierDetails by RequestId. Escalate to SME/ICM if no clue.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Glossary/Cannot import or export glossary term using template/Export terms using template
Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Known Issues/3P and MITI Data Sources Issues
symptom: MITI scan completes but assets are missing from scan results for 3P data sources (Snowflake, PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, etc.)
solution: Ask customer to run SELECT command on the missing asset using the same credentials configured in Purview scan. If SELECT fails, grant appropriate permissions to the scan identity.
rootCause: Purview scan user/credential does not have permission to access the missing assets in the data source

sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Known Issues/Azure Databricks Lineage and Authentication Issues
symptom: Azure Databricks lineage missing in Purview after scan completes; lineage tab shows no data
solution: (1) Verify lineage is from NOTEBOOK type. (2) Confirm within scan workspace scope. (3) Query DataScanAgentLinuxEvent for lineage result size. (4) If 0, ask customer to run lineage SQL on Databricks UI. (5) If Databricks has data but Purview does not, engage PG.
rootCause: Only NOTEBOOK entity type lineage is supported. Tables/notebooks must be in same workspace scope. system.access.table_lineage may have zero rows.

symptom: GET Glossaries API returns RequestTimeout error when listing glossary terms; large glossary causes single API call to exceed timeout
rootCause: Glossary contains too many terms/categories; fetching all in a single API call exceeds server-side timeout
solution: Add ignoreTermsAndCategories=true query parameter to GET glossary API; use separate List Terms and List Categories APIs with limit/offset pagination
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Glossary/Cannot import or export glossary term using template/Get Glossaries list failing with RequestTimeout error

symptom: Glossary CSV import fails: Some values of Term template names are not in [] or [Attribute][TermTemplateName]AttributeName should be empty
rootCause: Term Template in CSV does not exist in Purview; attribute names or field types mismatch
solution: Create Term Template manually in Purview portal matching CSV; column format: [Attribute][TermTemplateName]AttributeName. Retry import
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Glossary/Cannot import or export glossary term using template/Import terms failing | Expected column is missing from the template

symptom: Glossary term CSV import fails with duplicate term names warning
rootCause: Duplicate Name entries in CSV or term names contain unsupported special characters (#/%)
solution: Remove duplicate Name entries; use only letters/numbers/spaces/underscores/non-ASCII Unicode in term template names
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Glossary/Cannot import or export glossary term using template/Import terms using template | Duplicate or Transient Issue

symptom: HTTP 412 Pre-condition check failed when editing glossary term or entity in Purview portal
rootCause: Concurrent update on same entity; first request succeeds, subsequent fail with 412 ETag mismatch
solution: Click Cancel to exit edit, then Edit again to reload latest entity info and retry
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Glossary/Creating, editing or deleting glossary terms/Create, Update, Delete Errors

symptom: Azure Integration Runtime connection failures: timeout errors, authentication failures when connecting to data sources
rootCause: Incorrect credentials, network issues, or firewall/NSG settings blocking traffic from Azure IR to data source.
solution: 1) Verify credentials are correct. 2) Confirm data source is accessible from Azure IR. 3) Check NSG and firewall rules to allow traffic from the IR.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/IR Troubleshooting

symptom: Customer reports billing spike caused by Purview Insights reports; AtlasAnalytics job stuck in Submitting status (zombie job)
rootCause: AtlasAnalytics job hits the default Platform Job Service (PJS) timeout of 1440 minutes (24 hours) and the service does not return any status back to the caller, leaving the job stuck in Submitting state. This zombie job may cause unexpected billing charges.
solution: 1) Query JobInfoEvent to find AtlasAnalytics JobId and confirm status. 2) If result shows only Submitting with no Succeed/Failed row, customer is eligible for refund. 3) Engage CSS billing team for refund AND create ICM to insights team (use insights template in ASC) with exact timestamps of impact. 4) PG may need to collaborate with PJS ICM team for RCA.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Insights/Troubleshoot billing issues insights

symptom: Purview scan for Azure Files or ADLS Gen2 fails with Scan_TerminatedStuckScan error; scan runs for extended period then terminates
rootCause: Parser gets stuck on certain files (large or corrupt). Task slot remains in WaitingForActivation and never reaches RanToCompletion, causing the scan to be terminated after timeout.
solution: Use Kusto: 1) Check CustomerFacingEvent for Scan_TerminatedStuckScan. 2) Query GetScanAgentLinuxEvent to find stuck task slot (filter for WaitingForActivation status). 3) Find culprit file by checking "Put into taskslot" messages and URL-decode the file path. 4) If parser stuck: create bug with Area path Babylon\Connectors for DataSources oncall with ICM link + ScanRunId.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Known Issues/Azure Files, ADLS Gen2 Issues

symptom: Purview scan for Fabric Lakehouse sub-artifact metadata fails or returns incomplete results; error messages unclear or absent in diagnostic logs
rootCause: The associated Microsoft Fabric workspace is not assigned an active capacity. Without active capacity, metadata extraction for Lakehouse artifacts cannot complete.
solution: Ensure the affected Fabric workspace has an active capacity assigned. Refer to: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/admin/capacity-settings?tabs=power-bi-premium
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Known Issues/Fabric,Power BI Scan Issues TSG

symptom: Lineage not appearing for stored procedures invoked from Azure Data Factory (ADF) in Azure SQL DB lineage scan; SQL provenance scan does not capture RPC-based stored procedure execution
rootCause: Remote Procedure Calls (RPC) from ADF are not supported in SQL provenance lineage extraction. Only directly executed stored procedures are captured.
solution: Known limitation. Stored procedures invoked from ADF are not supported for lineage extraction. Only locally executed stored procedures show lineage. Prerequisites: DBOwner role in Azure SQL DB + run 'Create Master Key'. Lineage extraction runs every 6 hours (not configurable). Check latest documentation for updated support status.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Lineage/SQL Server Provenance, PowerBI sub-artifact lineage and PowerBI scan through VNET

symptom: Purview account deletion fails with error 'Failed to delete Purview account' in Azure Portal
rootCause: Azure resource lock applied at resource, resource group, or subscription scope prevents users from deleting the Purview account
solution: Remove the resource lock at the applicable scope (resource/resource group/subscription) via Azure Portal → Resource group → Locks, then retry deletion. Optionally verify via ARM logs: query HttpIncomingRequests union HttpOutgoingRequests filtering by subscriptionId and DELETE method targeting the Purview account, then use correlationId to check Traces table for detailed error.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Microsoft Purview Administration/Create or delete an Microsoft Purview instance/Azure resource lock preventing Microsoft Purview account deletion

symptom: Glossary terms are deleted directly without workflow approval when multi-selection (bulk delete) is applied. Single glossary deletion correctly shows Submit for Approval button, but selecting multiple glossaries shows a Delete button that bypasses the approval workflow.
rootCause: Product limitation — bulk operations on glossary terms do not trigger workflow approval checks. The workflow feature currently only supports single-item operations.
solution: Use Python SDK or JavaScript SDK with api_version="2024-01-11-preview" to submit user requests with multiple operations via /userrequests endpoint. Limitation: max 10 operations per request; only available in Python and JavaScript SDKs (not C# or Java).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Known Issues/Glossary Terms Deleted without approval from workflow when multi-selection is applied

symptom: View lineage not rendered after SQL DB metadata scan. Customer does not see view lineage for scanned SQL databases.
rootCause: If the asset was already ingested to data map before the lineage-enabled scan, the lineage will not appear on a new scan. Also MITI errors can prevent lineage extraction.
solution: Delete the asset from data map, then re-run the scan. If errors persist, check Kusto logs (DataScanAgentLinuxEvent for scan errors, TraceGatewayLocalEventLog for MITI errors) and escalate to connectors/engineering team.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Known Issues/SQL Server Lineage Issues

rootCause: Resource Provider unhealthy, Create/Delete Account Callback failure due to partner service issues, or ARM Endpoint unhealthy

Kusto: ProjectBabylonLogEvent | where TenantId == 'babylon-rp-prod-{region}' | search 'Attempting to transition'
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Microsoft Purview Administration/Create or delete an Microsoft Purview instance/Cannot Create Account

symptom: Policy publish allows binding policies to data sources that are not part of the asset being governed. Customer can associate policy with incorrect/unrelated data sources.
rootCause: Known product bug in policy publish flow — source validation does not properly restrict binding targets to only assets in the governed scope.
solution: PG is aware and working on a fix (ETA unknown). Advise customer that this is a known limitation and to carefully select the correct data sources when publishing.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy publish/FAQs and limitations

symptom: Created a Purview access policy but it is not getting enforced / policy not executing on the target data source.
rootCause: Policy is either not published, or was published to the wrong/incorrect data source.
solution: 1) Verify the policy status is 'Published'. 2) Verify the policy is published to the correct data source. 3) After publishing, wait at least 3 hours for policy replication to the Storage layer before testing.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy publish/Policy not executing

symptom: Policy deletion fails with error: 'Deletion of policy with bindings is not allowed'.
rootCause: One or more data sources are still associated (bound) with the policy. Policies with active bindings cannot be deleted.
solution: Delete all data source bindings (associations) from the policy first, then retry the delete operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Self-Service access policy deletion/Policy deletion failed

symptom: Customer reports unexpected extra charges on their AWS bill after running Purview scans on AWS S3 data sources.
rootCause: Purview scanning of AWS S3 incurs three categories of AWS-side charges: (1) data transfer fees for cross-region scanning, (2) S3 API call fees for LIST and GET requests, (3) KMS API fees for encrypted buckets.
solution: Explain the AWS cost components: 1) Data transfer: ~$0.02/GB for cross-region (US/EU). 2) S3 LIST requests: $0.0055/1K requests (1 request per 1K files). 3) S3 GET requests: $0.00044/1K requests (1 per classifiable file). 4) KMS Decrypt: $0.03/10K requests (1 per classifiable file in encrypted buckets). Refer to https://aws.amazon.com/s3/pricing/ and https://aws.amazon.com/kms/pricing/
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Purview Billing/AWS Extra charges on customer account

symptom: Duplicate assets in Purview Browse/Search: Azure SQL DB shown as Synapse Analytics, Azure Files appear in search despite deletion
rootCause: Known Search team issue: entity type deduplication not working correctly in Browse and Search (tracked by Feature 1022062)
solution: No fix available yet. Workaround: use REST API to manually delete stale assets.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Known Issues/Product Specific & General Known Issues

solution: Disable FIPS on SHIR: Local Security Policy -> Security Options -> System cryptography: FIPS -> Disabled. Restart SHIR. Kusto: TraceGatewayLocalEventLog | where LocalMessage contains 'FIPS validated cryptographic algorithms'

rootCause: SHIR machine lacks network connectivity to Purview endpoint; firewall blocking
solution: Add firewall rules for SHIR to reach Purview. Check copy activity output -> reportLineageToCatalog -> status.

rootCause: ADF-Purview connection not established correctly; adcProperties show empty/wrong catalog URI
solution: Verify ADF-Purview connection per docs. Try disconnect/reconnect Synapse. Kusto: DataflowClusterLogs | where ActivityRunId == '<id>' | where Caller contains 'lineage'
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Lineage/Cannot see full lineage from Azure Synapse Analytics

rootCause: Purview uses Private Endpoints but ADF uses public Azure IR, or SHIR VNet cannot reach Purview PE; DNS misconfiguration

symptom: Purview account deployment fails with Conflict error due to Storage Account or EventHub namespace quota limits exceeded in the subscription
rootCause: Microsoft Purview creates managed resources (Storage Account, EventHub Namespace) in the subscription. If customer has exceeded limits on blob storage or EventHub, the deployment fails with a Conflict error.
solution: Check if customer has exceeded Storage or EventHub limits. If exceeded, suggest deleting unused resources or submitting a quota increase request. Verify prerequisites: register resource providers Microsoft.EventHub, Microsoft.Purview, Microsoft.Storage.

rootCause: Azure portal marketplace rendering issue prevents direct Purview account creation via the standard Create flow.

rootCause: The data factory service principal was removed from the Purview root collection's Data Curator role, either accidentally or by another admin
solution: 1) Go to Purview → Collection page → check if the data factory is still listed as a Data Curator in the root collection. 2) If removed, re-add the data factory to root collection as Data Curator. 3) Verify via Purview API: PUT metadataPolicies endpoint + PATCH data factory ARM to set purviewConfiguration.purviewResourceId
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Lineage/Connecting to lineage sources/Troubleshoot Data factory disappears or gets disconnected on Purview portal

symptom: Purview account creation fails preflight validation due to invalid account name
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Microsoft Purview Administration/Create or delete an Microsoft Purview instance/Validation Failures
rootCause: Account name does not meet naming requirements: must be 3-63 characters, contain only alphanumeric characters and dashes, and every dash must be immediately preceded and followed by a letter or number
solution: Verify account name meets requirements: 3-63 characters, alphanumeric + dash only, no leading/trailing dash, no consecutive dashes. Correct the name and retry.

symptom: Customer asks how to calculate VCoreHour consumption for a specific Purview scan
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Microsoft Purview Administration/How To/How-To FAQs
solution: VCoreHour = numOfWorkers x defaultVCorePerWorker x scanRunTime(hours). numOfWorkers = CUs specified at account creation. defaultVCorePerWorker is approx 8 but dynamic at runtime based on data source size. ScanRunTime in hours (15 min = 0.25). Note: formula is approximate; exact value depends on runtime VCore allocation.

symptom: Purview account creation fails with error: 2005 - The Tenant with xx resources has surpassed its resource quota 20 for resource type Account in [location]
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Microsoft Purview Administration/I need to increase a quota limit/Error 2005 - The Tenant with xx resources has surpassed its resource quota
rootCause: Tenant has reached the maximum 20 Purview account quota per region. Each region has its own quota per tenant.
solution: 1) Run Kusto query on babylon.eastus2.kusto.windows.net/babylonMdsLogs: AccountInfo | where tenantId == <TenantID> | where location == <Region> | project SubscriptionId, tenantId, AccountId, CatalogId, creationTime, location, AccountName, provisioningState. 2) Identify unwanted accounts and remove them, or provision in another region. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/purview/how-to-manage-quotas

symptom: User cannot access ADLS Gen2 or Azure Blob Storage asset from Storage Explorer, PowerBI Desktop, or Azure Portal after creating a Reader policy at file/asset level via Purview Data Policy
rootCause: Purview Reader policy at file level uses ABAC (Attribute-Based Access Control), not ACL. Client applications like Storage Explorer, PBI Desktop, and Azure Portal require at least container-level access to list items and query data. File-level read policy alone is insufficient for browsing.
solution: 1) For browse access via Storage Explorer/PBI: create policy at container level instead of file level. 2) For file-level access only: use Azure Synapse SQL script (OPENROWSET), Synapse Notebook (ABFS command), or direct Synapse asset search. Requires: Contributor + Synapse Administrator + Synapse SQL Administrator on workspace, Data reader on Purview account, Purview connected to Synapse.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Data access when Reader policy is created at specific asset level (via Data policy in Microsoft Purview)

symptom: ADLS Gen2 data policy created via Purview Data Governance is not working as expected
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: 1) Policy created at file level instead of container level 2) Wrong authentication method (must use AAD) 3) Policy not yet replicated to storage account (~3hrs delay) 4) Storage account ACL takes precedence over Purview Policy
solution: 1) For Storage Explorer: create policy at container level. 2) For Synapse workspace: file-level policy should work. 3) Ensure AAD authentication with required permissions. 4) Wait ~3hrs for policy replication. 5) Check and correct storage account ACLs as they take precedence over Purview Policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy - Customer Issues/ADLS Gen2 policy not working

symptom: AuthenticationFailedException 'DefaultAzureCredential authentication failed due to an unhandled exception' in Purview Policy Store logs
rootCause: Bad node in the pod cannot connect to managed identity; issue observed primarily in Canary environment
solution: Follow steps to delete the bad node that cannot connect to managed identity (see linked guide: Pod cannot connect to managed identity)
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy - Customer Issues/Bad node throwing authentication failed errors

symptom: Policy Store calls to Artifact Store fail with 'ArtifactStoreError_PurviewAuthzPolicy' and 'ArtifactStoreErrorCode: DataFactoryDoesNotExist'
rootCause: The data factory associated with the Purview account does not exist in Artifact Store
solution: Contact RP team to investigate why the data factory for the Purview account does not exist. Query PolicyStoreLogEvent with ExceptionType 'ArtifactStoreException' to get details.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy - Customer Issues/Collection reference not available in Artifact Store

symptom: Customer cannot create or publish a policy in Purview Data Governance
rootCause: Data governance flag not enabled on data source, or user lacks Policy Author role (not assigned by default), or user lacks Data Source Admin role required for publishing
solution: 1) Enable data governance flag on the data source. 2) Assign Policy Author role to the user for creating policies. 3) Assign Data Source Admin role for publishing policies. Consult administrators for role assignments.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy - Customer Issues/Customer is not able to create and publish a policy

symptom: Policy Store CRUD operations fail with ErrorResponseException/ArtifactStoreException 'The reference does not exist or is not valid in artifact store'
rootCause: Collection reference has not yet propagated to Artifact Store (timing/propagation delay)
solution: Retries should succeed once propagation completes. Check with RP team if the collection is in a failed state. Query PolicyStoreLogEvent for ExceptionType 'ErrorResponseException' or 'ArtifactStoreException'.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy - Customer Issues/Failing Artifact Store requests

symptom: 500 Internal Server Error from Gateway during policy CRUD operations, or 401 Unauthorized when Policy Store clients attempt to connect
rootCause: Client certificate domain used is not whitelisted in the environment-specific configuration (region YAML file)
solution: 1) Get correlation ID from logs and query ProjectBabylonLogEvent. 2) Check the certificate domain from logs against whitelisted domains in the region-specific YAML config. 3) If domain not whitelisted, ask reporter to use appropriate domain. 4) If domain is correct, escalate to SME.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy - Customer Issues/Invalid Client Certificate Issues TSG

symptom: Customer needs to migrate glossaries, glossary terms, or metadata from classic Azure Purview Data Catalog to the new Data Governance experience
rootCause: The new Data Governance experience underwent a complete overhaul of glossary terms/templates; no built-in migration path provided by Microsoft engineering
solution: Recommend the official third-party tool Macula Purview Automate (https://www.maculasys.com/microsoft-purview) which supports migrating glossaries, governance domains, data products, and data assets. CSS will NOT support this tool — redirect customer to Macula support directly for any issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Apps - Data Governance/Migration to the new experience

symptom: Old Purview accounts throw error: cnfCondition in AttributeRule not supported. PolicyStoreException in logs with message containing cnfCondition in AttributeRule.
rootCause: Validation rules were updated but old accounts have not been migrated, causing unsupported cnfCondition in attribute rules.
solution: Account needs migration. Query PolicyStoreLogEvent on babylon.eastus2 cluster for ExceptionType PolicyStoreException and cnfCondition to identify affected accounts. Contact Policy Store team SME for migration details.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy - Customer Issues/Old accounts throwing errors

symptom: 401 Error from policystore API in collection/role permissions. Caller not authorized with missing Microsoft.Purview/accounts/policy/write permission. TIP test PolicyStoreTest in region failing.
rootCause: Caller does not have the required Microsoft.Purview/accounts/policy/write permission. Metadata roles or Data roles may need to be created or updated in the global DB across environments (Dev/DF/Canary/Prod).
solution: 1) Get correlation ID from DGrep/Jarvis logs. 2) Query ProjectBabylonLogEvent with CorrelationId to confirm unauthorized caller. 3) Create PR for role changes in Policy Store repo. 4) Update roles via Geneva Actions: Project Babylon > Babylon Generic Json Resource Operations > Create or Update Generic JSON Resource. 5) Update in order: Dev/DF (Test env) -> Canary -> Prod (Public env). 6) For feature-flagged roles, update exposure-control role entity first. Escalate to @pragarwal or @desinghal if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy - Customer Issues/Roles update request and permission issue

symptom: ADLS Gen2 container not visible in Asset section when creating a new policy in Purview
rootCause: Data source or container has not been scanned successfully, or Purview account lacks access to the container
solution: 1) Verify the data source/container has been scanned successfully - if not, rescan it. 2) Check if Purview account has access to the container by performing Test Connection. 3) If access is missing, provide appropriate access through Key Access or AAD.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy Creation/ADLS Gen2 container is not visible

symptom: Purview access policy not taking effect even after waiting 2 hours
rootCause: Storage account created outside supported regions - initially only France Central and Canada Central were supported for policy enforcement on Azure Storage
solution: Verify the storage account is in a supported region. If not, create a new storage account in a supported region (check latest supported regions at https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/purview/how-to-access-policies-storage#azure-storage-enforcement-side). Policies may take up to 2 hours to propagate.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Policy/Policy Creation/Supported sources and FAQs

symptom: Customer billed for a scan that did not complete successfully (Classic Billing Model)
rootCause: If a scan fails after a partial run, the customer is still charged for the vCore hours consumed during the partial execution
solution: Check the 'Assets discovered' field for the mentioned scan run ID. If non-zero, some assets were ingested and the charge is valid. This applies to the Classic/Old billing model only.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Purview Billing/Customer billed for an incomplete scan(Classic Billing Model)

symptom: Purview Ingestion Service billing charges unexpectedly high; customer does not have many scanning jobs but sees large ingestion quantity in BillingLogEvent
rootCause: ADF, Synapse, or Power BI pushing lineage information to Purview without customer awareness, causing high ingestion volume
solution: Verify ingestion source in Purview Studio (Data Map > Monitor links). If lineage is the cause, disconnect the Synapse/ADF connection to Purview. Use BillingLogEvent Kusto query filtered by Component=Ingestion Service to confirm charges. Query GatewayEvent with IngestionJobId from AdditionalProperties to identify source (DataFactory/Synapse/etc.)
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Purview Billing/Billing Refund/Look Up Billing Charges

symptom: Salesforce scan fails in Purview; test connection or credential errors when scanning Salesforce data source
rootCause: Salesforce connector uses REST API with app created in Salesforce (not Azure). Credential requires 2 Key Vault secrets: (1) user password (or password+security token if IP not trusted), (2) consumer key with consumer secret. Misconfiguration of these secrets or Salesforce app permissions/IP restrictions causes failures
solution: Verify Salesforce app has OAuth enabled and correct IP restrictions. Check 2 KV secrets: Secret 1 = user password (append security token if SHIR IP not in Salesforce trusted ranges), Secret 2 = consumer key + consumer secret. Test with cURL: curl -v -k https://[ENDPOINT]/services/oauth2/token -d grant_type=password -d client_id=[KEY] -d client_secret=[SECRET] -d username=[USER] -d password=[PWD]. If cURL fails → Salesforce config issue; if succeeds → Purview credential issue
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/3P Data Source Salesforce

symptom: Asset deleted from data source is not automatically removed from Purview catalog after subsequent scan
rootCause: Scan scope is set to individual items (tables/files) rather than their direct parent or higher level. Auto-deletion detection requires the scope to include the direct parent folder/container or higher in the hierarchy
solution: Adjust scan scope to the direct parent level or higher (e.g., database level for SQL, parent folder for storage). Look for a checkbox/tick icon at parent level instead of square icon (partial selection). After adjusting scope and rescanning, deletion takes ~6 hours to reflect in catalog. Verify scan traversal with Kusto: DataScanAgentLinuxEvent | where ScanResultId == "<id>" | where Message has "OpInfo" | extract URI pattern
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Asset deletion auto detect

symptom: AWS S3 test connection fails with error about missing external ID in AWS role configuration
rootCause: AWS IAM role trust relationship does not include the ExternalId condition required by Purview credential
solution: Go to Purview credential page, copy External ID. In AWS IAM, Roles, select role, Trust relationships, edit, add Condition: {StringEquals: {sts:ExternalId: copied-id}}. Save and retest.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating Data sources/AWS S3 test connection failures

symptom: Purview scan credential test fails with Key Vault authentication issues; misconfigured Key Vault URI, secret name, or MSI permissions
rootCause: Multiple possible misconfigurations: wrong KV URI, Purview MSI lacks Get/List secrets, secret name mismatch, or SP missing data source permissions
solution: Verify: (1) Key Vault URI matches actual AKV, (2) Purview MSI has Get+List secret permissions in AKV Access Policies, (3) Secret name in credential matches KV secret exactly, (4) SP has correct data source permissions. If unresolved, escalate via ICM.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating Data sources/KeyVault Auth Issues

symptom: Purview cannot connect to Key Vault behind private endpoint; AKV connection issues in private network
rootCause: Purview communicates with Key Vault directly (NOT through SHIR). AKV behind VNET requires Trusted Services enabled on firewall
solution: Enable 'Allow trusted Microsoft services to bypass this firewall' in AKV Firewall settings. Do NOT look for SHIR logs for KV issues (Purview-KV traffic bypasses SHIR). For PG escalation provide: AKV firewall screenshot, AKV logs, SHIR logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating Data sources/Keyvault and private endpoints

symptom: Purview (private network) scan of Azure Synapse dedicated pool (public endpoint) fails with Internal system error when using Auto-resolve IR
rootCause: Auto-resolve IR cannot connect from a private-network Purview to a public-endpoint Synapse; requires Self-hosted IR with proper RBAC (Reader on workspace, Storage Blob Data Reader) and SQL login/user created for Purview MSI
solution: 7-step fix: (1) Add Purview MSI as Reader on Synapse workspace IAM, (2) Grant Storage Blob Data Reader on Synapse resource group/subscription, (3) CREATE LOGIN for Purview in Synapse serverless SQL, (4) CREATE USER + db_datareader on each database, (5) Set up Self-hosted IR, (6) Create Key Vault (whitelisted or same VNet as SHIR), (7) Create credential in Purview using Key Vault
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating or using Credentials/Connect Purview (Private Network) to Azure Synapse Workspace (Public Network)

symptom: Purview scan test connection fails with "Failed to access the [Data Source] account with the given credentials" when using Key Vault authentication
rootCause: Key Vault firewall blocking Purview access, VNET misconfiguration, incorrect credential/secret selection, access policy not configured for Purview, Key Vault Access Control misconfigured, or secret expiration
solution: Verify 7 items: (1) Key Vault has no firewall blocking Purview, (2) VNET config allows connectivity, (3) Correct secret with account key value is selected, (4) Access policy grants Purview MSI Get/List on secrets, (5) Access Control (RBAC) is correct, (6) Secret has not expired, (7) Review detailed scan logs. If all checks pass, open ICM with screenshots.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating or using Credentials/Key Vault Authentication

symptom: Purview scan test-connectivity fails; general debugging approach using Kusto ScanningLog queries and correlation IDs to diagnose credential and connectivity issues (20501/20502/20503 errors)
rootCause: Most common: credentials not properly added to AKV (mistyped key), or Purview MSI not granted access to Azure Key Vault. Errors 20501/20502/20503 indicate AKV access issues (can take 10 min for ACLs to propagate)
solution: Use Kusto queries: (1) Find accountId via ScanningLog where Message contains account name, (2) Find correlation IDs via ScanningLog where Uri contains /testConnectivity, (3) Debug with ScanningLog filtered by CorrelationId. Verify credential setup per authentication method (account key, SP, SQL auth).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating Data sources/Test Connection Issues

symptom: ADLS Gen 2 scan with MSI authentication fails when storage account firewall is enabled; connection fails with firewall + whitelisted client IP, scan fails with firewall + Purview public IP
rootCause: ADLS Gen 2 Storage RP MSI resource match is case-sensitive and does not match the MSI details Purview provides. MSI auth only works when 'all networks' access is enabled.
solution: Use Account Key authentication instead of MSI when ADLS Gen 2 firewall is enabled. MSI+all networks works for both connection and scan. With firewall enabled, MSI fails regardless of IP whitelisting.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating or using Credentials/Product Specific Security (MSI, AKV, PE, SHIR, VNET)

symptom: Azure Databricks connection issues after metastore ID migration; ADB scan or connection not working after November 2024 update
rootCause: ADB connection was updated to include metastore ID in FQN. All accounts migrated starting November 15. Some connections need manual refresh to pick up new metastore info.
solution: Navigate to ADB Connection under Connection tab, update the description field (any change), and click Save. This action refreshes the metastore information on the backend.

symptom: Hive source scan returns partial/oscillating asset count across runs; not all assets discovered

symptom: Classifications missing on schema for delimited file types (CSV/TSV/SSV/PSV) or Parquet file assets; classifications assigned to tabular_schema but not displayed in UX
rootCause: File asset reported by both Scan and ADF. Scan reports schema to _tabular_schema_, ADF reports to _attachedSchema_. UX displays _attachedSchema_ which lacks auto-classifications from Scan
solution: Request enabling _attachedSchema_ feature via IcM (title: [HierarchicalSchema Enabling] for Account Name: [XXX] by [A Date]); re-scan after enabling; use Microsoft-provided migration tool to move curations from old schema
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Missing classification on schema

rootCause: Microsoft Purview does not have a fixed client IP range to whitelist in SQL Server firewall rules
solution: Enable 'Allow Azure services and resources to access this server' option in the SQL Server firewall settings (Azure Portal > SQL Server > Networking)

rootCause: Microsoft Purview does not support query or stored procedure for lineage or scanning of Oracle sources. Lineage is limited to table and view sources only.
solution: Inform customer this is a known limitation. Purview only supports table and view sources for Oracle scanning and lineage. Stored procedures are not supported.

rootCause: Misconfiguration of Purview-PowerBI integration: Purview and PowerBI not in same tenant, service principal not properly configured, or security group membership incorrect
solution: Verify: (1) Purview and PowerBI are in the same Azure tenant, (2) Customer followed setup doc (register-scan-power-bi-tenant), (3) Power BI admin portal has 'Allow service principal to use read-only Power BI admin APIs' enabled, (4) AAD Security Group has correct Purview MSI as member and type is Security. Collect ScanningLog traces with scan run ID for further diagnosis.

rootCause: Connectivity issue between scan executor and configuration blob storage, preventing reading/decrypting scan config
solution: Investigate connectivity issues. If using Self-Hosted IR (SHIR), check SHIR connectivity troubleshooting guide. Verify network connectivity between SHIR and Purview backend services.

solution: Upgrade SHIR to version > 5.8.7866.8 to mitigate the issue
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Scan fails with an error/Scan failed with error: Content: Catalog Not Found, or is an invalid state

solution: Uninstall current SHIR and install latest version from https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=39717

rootCause: Missing permissions: MSI needs Reader on resource group/subscription, Storage Blob Data Reader on Synapse storage account, and specific SQL user creation for serverless/dedicated pools
solution: Ensure all permissions: (1) MSI Reader on RG/subscription, (2) Storage Blob Data Reader on Synapse storage, (3) For serverless SQL: create user per docs, (4) For dedicated SQL: follow MSI setup steps, (5) If enumeration works but scan fails, repeat serverless SQL user creation steps

symptom: Scan executor task stuck in queued state for days, scan never starts or completes
rootCause: Insufficient capacity in the Azure Batch Pool backing the scan executor; not enough compute nodes available to schedule the task
solution: Use Azure Batch Diagnostics tool (https://azurebatchdiagnostics.azurewebsites.net/) to check task status with the activity ID. Compare AddedSuccessfully vs ScheduledSuccessfully timestamps. If large gap, the issue is insufficient Batch Pool capacity — check pool node count and scale settings. If scheduling is prompt, investigate further in ADMS

solution: Upgrade Integration Runtime to version 5.1.7655.1 or higher. Verify DataScan agent version >= 252 in nuspec.xml
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Scan fails with an error/Unable to complete scan of on-premise SQL. Data connection tested successfully

symptom: Assets not visible in Browse page after scan completes; customer clicks 'Browse by Asset Type' but cannot find server/account/top-level assets
rootCause: Scoped scans have a 3-4 hour delay before assets appear in Browse; full scans should show assets immediately after completion
solution: Use Search functionality (keyword + filters) as interim workaround. For scoped scans, wait 3-4 hours. If assets don't appear after 6 hours, file CRI to Data Map team. For ADLSGen1/Gen2/Azure File/Blob/AWS S3 offline job issues, transfer ticket to Offline Tier team. Use Kusto query on DataScanAgentEvent to check if scan was full or scoped.

symptom: Search returns HTTP 400 error 'The search request is invalid' when searching keywords
rootCause: Search keywords contain special characters (/, +, -, {, etc.) that are treated as complex query syntax, causing syntax errors
solution: Remove all symbol characters from the search keyword or double-quote the keyword. Use Kusto (OnlineTierDetails with RequestId) to find actual keywords used. If customer needs complex query or result is still unexpected, file CRI to PG team with account name, request ID, target asset, and keyword used.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Search and Browse/Cannot search or browse assets after a successful scan/Browse failure

symptom: Search by column name does not return column entities in search results; searching 'address' returns entities containing 'address' in content but not the column named 'address'
rootCause: Keyword search matches content within columns but not column names themselves; column names are properties of parent entities (e.g., parquet files) and are not indexed as separate searchable entities (tracked by PBI 1135273)
solution: Known limitation. Search for the parent entity (e.g., the parquet file or table) instead of the column name directly. Column names appear as properties of the parent entity in search results.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Search and Browse/Known Issues (Search & Browse Purview)

symptom: Search does not return expected assets when searching by keyword or applying filters
rootCause: Multiple causes: camelCase asset names not matched by partial lowercase keywords; words >10 chars need wildcard; special characters in keywords treated as query syntax; low relevance score pushing results to deep pages; asset may not be searchable
solution: Adjust search keywords: split camelCase names (e.g., 'product detail' instead of 'productde'), append '*' for wildcard on words >10 chars (e.g., 'productdetail*'), remove or double-quote special characters. For other cases, file CRI to PG with account name, request ID, target asset, and keyword used.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Search and Browse/Search results does not return an asset/Search result does not return expected asset

rootCause: Browser/device compliance policy blocks access in Incognito mode where compliance extensions are disabled by default
solution: Ensure device enrolled. Allow popups for web.purview.azure.com. Install latest OS updates. For Chrome/Edge install Windows 10 accounts extension and enable in Incognito.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Opening Microsoft Purview Governance Portal/Browser Compat and Incognito mode

rootCause: Known issue - error 500 when user logged into multiple Purview accounts while not being owner

sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Opening Microsoft Purview Governance Portal/Request failed with status code 500

symptom: Portal Private Endpoint not working after Purview GA: customer cannot open Purview portal in VNET; deprecated PE DNS zone privatelink.purview.azure.com
rootCause: Portal PE created before Purview GA uses deprecated DNS zone privatelink.purview.azure.com. After GA the correct zone is privatelink.purviewstudio.azure.com.
solution: 1) Check if private DNS zone is privatelink.purview.azure.com (deprecated). 2) Delete current portal PE. 3) Create new portal PE with DNS integration -> new zone privatelink.purviewstudio.azure.com. 4) If custom DNS, update record from web.privatelink.purview.azure.com to web.privatelink.purviewstudio.azure.com. 5) Verify with nslookup web.purview.azure.com.

symptom: Cannot access Purview portal with Private Endpoints when using proxy: error says account is behind PE, please access from client in same VNet
rootCause: Customer outbound traffic routes through corporate proxy, Purview portal sees proxy IP instead of VNET private IP.
solution: 1) Identify proxy via F12 Network tab Remote Address. 2) Edit SHIR diahost.exe.config and diawp.exe.config. 3) Add bypasslist entries for managed EventHub (.servicebus.windows.net), storage (.blob.core.windows.net, .queue.core.windows.net), and web.purview.azure.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Private Endpoints/Proxy considerations while using SHIR along with Private endpoints

symptom: Browser cache conflict causes Not Authorized error after enabling Private Endpoints on Purview account
rootCause: Stale browser cache from pre-PE session conflicts with post-PE state causing authorization failure.
solution: Perform browser hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) or open Purview account in new tab/InPrivate window from within the VNet configured for Account + Portal PE.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Private Endpoints/Troubleshooting Network Connection

symptom: 403 Not Authorized when accessing Purview portal with Private Endpoints even when client is in same VNET
rootCause: Missing Account PE and/or Portal PE configuration. Custom DNS A records for account and portal domains not properly configured.
solution: 1) Create both Account PE and Portal PE matching VNET/subnet. 2) Custom DNS: create privatelink.purview.azure.com zone with A records for Web and AccountName. 3) Add DNS conditional forwarding for purview.azure.com to 168.63.129.16. 4) Add ingestion PE DNS for blob, queue, servicebus. 5) Verify with nslookup and Test-NetConnection port 443.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Private Endpoints/Troubleshooting Network Connection

symptom: Cannot add tags on Purview account: HTTP 409 error with deny assignment and policy violation on managed resources
rootCause: Azure Policies block tag addition on Purview managed resources (managed RG storage account and eventhub namespace). Tags on Purview account flow to managed resources, and if policies block tag modifications on those resources, operation fails.
solution: 1) Collect browser HAR file. 2) Look for POST to management.azure.com/batch failing with 409. 3) Run Kusto: RpHttpApiRequests | where AccountName==accountname | where HttpMethod==PATCH to get CorrelationId. 4) Run ProjectBabylonLogs | where CorrelationId in (id) to identify blocking policy. 5) Engage Azure Policy team to help customer modify/exempt the blocking policy for managed resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Unable to add tags on Purview account

symptom: Snowflake scan URL mismatch: JDBC driver translates underscores to hyphens in Snowflake account URL causing connection failure
rootCause: Snowflake JDBC driver version 3.13.25+ behavior change translates underscores (_) to hyphens (-) in account identifiers, causing DNS resolution failure for privatelink URLs.
solution: Create DNS entry for the hyphenated version to match the original underscore version (e.g. DNS for indusind-edp-nonprod.privatelink.snowflakecomputing.com pointing to indusind-edp_nonprod.privatelink.snowflakecomputing.com). Prerequisites: JDK 11 + Visual Studio C++ on SHIR machine.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Snowflake troubleshooting

sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/Microsoft Purview: Merging Accounts - Troubleshooting Guide
symptom: Merge button is not present in Purview despite having the right permissions. Customer cannot merge Purview accounts.
rootCause: An active merge workflow exists under Workflow -> Authoring (named "Merge Purview account" or "Merge Purview Account from Secondary Account") that blocks the merge button from appearing.
solution: 1) Check Data Map -> Monitoring -> Account merging for merge status. 2) Check logs in babylonMdsLogs ProjectBabylonLogEvent for merge request messages. 3) If nothing found, check Workflow -> Authoring for active merge workflows and have customer cancel/reject them. The merge button should reappear. Note: merge is irreversible once completed.

sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/Microsoft Purview: Merging Accounts - Troubleshooting Guide
symptom: Purview account merge fails with conflicts: scan rule sets with same name, ADF/Synapse connections in both accounts, term templates with same name, or lineage scans in secondary account.
rootCause: Technical limitations: 1) Scan rule set/term template names must be unique per account (used as internal identifiers). 2) ADF/Synapse connections are per-account, not per-domain, and user may lack Owner/Contributor on ADF. 3) Lineage scans depend on ADF connection which has same limitation.
solution: Resolve conflicts before merge: rename duplicate scan rule sets/term templates in one account, remove duplicate ADF/Synapse connections, remove lineage scans from secondary account. Ref: learn.microsoft.com/purview/merge-domain

sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/New Purview Unified Portal scenarios/FAQs for account upgrade, free-tier and enterprise-tier
symptom: Customer unable to upgrade Purview from Free-Tier to Enterprise-Tier in GCC M365 tenant.
rootCause: Purview Data Governance app is not supported in GCC M365 Tenants. Upgrade is blocked for GCC environments.
solution: Verify GCC tenant by calling https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/.well-known/openid-configuration and checking tenant_region_sub_scope field for "GCC" value. Advise customer GCC is not supported. Ref: learn.microsoft.com/purview/new-portal-faq

sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/New Purview Unified Portal scenarios/FAQs for account upgrade, free-tier and enterprise-tier
symptom: Customer chose wrong Purview account for enterprise upgrade. Needs to switch to a different account.
rootCause: Upgrade process once completed cannot be rolled back. Customer did not verify which account should be promoted.
solution: Contact Purview PG to reconcile to a different account. Provide: tenant ID, current (incorrect) enterprise account, correct enterprise account details. Upgrade is irreversible but PG can promote a different account in exceptional cases.

symptom: Customer cannot see Purview account in Azure Portal after upgrade to enterprise account.
rootCause: User lacks permissions to view the enterprise account. Subscription-level permissions are not related to enterprise upgrade; only root collection admin or account owner permissions matter.
solution: Verify user permissions per learn.microsoft.com/purview/catalog-permissions. User needs to request access from root collection admin or account owner. Check root collection admin via Data Map -> Collections.

solution: Follow TSG for region change. Free tier can move between supported regions but DATA WILL BE LOST. For enterprise in unsupported region, customer needs business justification. Do NOT create ICM without following TSG first. Escalate to EEEs for exceptions.

solution: Options: 1) Create separate tenants for dev and prod, each with their own Purview account. 2) Use Collections within single account to logically separate dev/prod. Discuss with EEEs if customer insists on multiple accounts.

symptom: Customer wants to rename Purview account. Cannot rename account after creation.
solution: Classic portal: Set a friendly name via Purview Studio → Management tab → Change friendly name. Note: actual account name does not change, only display name in Purview Studio. New portal (purview.microsoft.com): not applicable as it operates at tenant level. Renameable components: Domain name (yes), Collection name (yes), Workflow name (yes), Business/Physical assets (yes). symptom: Azure Purview upgrade to enterprise fails due to region mismatch between existing free tier account and intended enterprise tier. Free tier was auto-provisioned in a region based on M365 tenant home location (EOP) → PowerBI tenant location → default mapping, and customer selected a different region for enterprise.
rootCause: Free tier account exists in a different region than the region chosen for enterprise upgrade. The system follows a precedence order: M365 tenant (EOP) home location → PowerBI tenant location → default mapping. When customer chooses a region different from the free tier location, upgrade fails.
solution: 1) Use Kusto query on babylonMdsLogs to find existing free tier accounts: _AccountInfoSnapshotEvent | where time > ago(2h) | where TenantId == '{tenantId}' | where Sku contains 'Free' | distinct TenantId, Sku, CreatedAt, Location. 2) If customer wants same-GEO region change (e.g. North Europe→West Europe): auto-approved, raise ICM to MSG Tenant Provisioning → Customer Escalation & Engagement. 3) Cross-GEO not allowed except for compliance/regulatory reasons. 4) Free tier accounts cannot be self-deleted by customers; deletion requires support request to Purview Platform DRI team.

symptom: Azure Purview account merge failure when merging classic Azure Purview account into Enterprise Account (Unified Purview). Or customer needs to un-reconcile accounts after reconciling wrong account for testing, then wants to reconcile production account instead.
rootCause: Merge or reconcile operation may fail due to configuration limits or wrong account selection during the upgrade/reconcile process.
solution: 1) Merge failure: No PM approval required as long as within supported configuration bounds (max merge limit). Raise ICM directly. 2) Un-reconcile: Raise ICM to un-reconcile (no PM approval needed). Warn customer about DG layer data loss. 3) After un-reconcile, verify billing model is correct — check if pointing to NEW MSP billing (assets protected/governed) or classic billing (CU/vCores). 4) For any doubts, check with EEEs/Leads in AVA before contacting PM directly.

symptom: Schema of an asset is missing after successful scan due to sampling failure. Purview attempts to sample 128 rows per column; if the sampling query fails (e.g., data files not found in serverless DB pointing to ADLS Gen2), schema will not be available.
rootCause: Purview samples 128 rows per column during scan. If the underlying data source is inaccessible during sampling, the sampling query fails and schema is not populated. This is by design.
solution: 1) Verify the data files referenced by the source exist and are accessible. 2) If data files are missing/moved, fix the source data. 3) This is by-design behavior - Purview requires successful sampling to populate schema.

symptom: Asset schema or entire table missing from Purview catalog after scan due to entity payload exceeding size limits (1MB payload, 800 columns, 700k characters).
rootCause: Purview has hard limits: 1MB max payload, 800 columns max, 700k+ characters. When entity exceeds these, Purview drops it entirely. CSV requires >= 3 columns and 1MB limit may cut inside a column.
solution: 1) Check if asset has too many columns (>800) or large schema. 2) Short-term PG fix: truncate schema instead of dropping entity. 3) Mid-term: increase threshold. 4) Scan limits: structured files sample 128 rows or 1MB (lower); document files sample 20MB max; CosmosDB reads top 10 docs.

symptom: Deleted asset still visible in Purview catalog after running scan. Scan scope was not set to include the direct parent of the deleted asset or higher.
rootCause: Scan scope must be configured to the DIRECT PARENT or higher level of the deleted asset for deletion to be detected. Scan must be triggered after asset is deleted from source.
solution: 1) Set scan scope to include direct parent folder/container of deleted asset (or higher). 2) Trigger full scan after deletion. 3) Verify using DataScanAgentLinuxEvent with OpInfo/EntityInfo to check traversed assets.

solution: Do NOT immediately request CU upgrade. Investigation steps: 1) Check SHIR memory utilization, 2) Assess data volume, 3) Check for DB throttling/blocking, 4) Investigate network issues. Work with SME/PG and complete investigation in AVA first. Only raise IcM for CU increase if root cause is confirmed as CU limit. Require business impact justification from customer.

rootCause: CU downgrade functionality is not implemented in the platform
solution: Inform customer that decreasing CU limit is NOT supported. Advise caution: once CU is upgraded, it cannot be reversed. Ensure thorough investigation confirms CU is truly the bottleneck before approving any upgrade request.
