symptom: Purview account creation or deletion fails with Conflict error and RequestDisallowedByPolicy in Azure Portal; Azure Policy blocks operations on managed resources (Storage Account, EventHub Namespace)
rootCause: Azure Policy rules in the customer subscription deny create/update operations on managed resources (Storage Account, EventHub Namespace) in the Purview managed resource group. Purview creates these managed resources automatically and they must pass all policy checks.
solution: 1. Identify the blocking policy from Activity Logs or Kusto: cluster('babylon.eastus2').database('babylonMdsLogs').ProjectBabylonLogEvent | where * contains 'accountname' | where TracingLevel == 'Error'. 2. Ask customer to modify policy definition to allow managed resources, OR apply specific tags on the Purview account (tags propagate automatically to managed resources, granting policy exception). 3. Customer can create a policy exemption for the managed resource group.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Microsoft Purview Administration/Create or delete an Microsoft Purview instance/Cannot Delete Account

symptom: Scan log queries using OpInfo keyword return no results after Microsoft removed OpInfo structure from Purview scan logs
rootCause: Microsoft phased out the OpInfo keyword from scan logs across all regions (completed by June 2023) and replaced it with ConvertedEntities
solution: Replace OpInfo with ConvertedEntities in all Kusto scan log queries. The ConvertedEntities keyword provides the same functionality including samplingLabel, isLeafNode, qualifiedName etc.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Check scan logs using "ConvertedEntities" instead of "OpInfo"

symptom: Users unable to access Purview Governance Portal despite having Owner, Data Curator, Data Source Admin, and Reader roles assigned via IAM. Error: You do not have access to this Purview account. Contact an Owner or User Access Administrator (UAA) for access in Access control (IAM). Account was created before August 2021 and uses legacy Azure RBAC instead of collection-based access.
rootCause: Purview accounts created before 18 August 2021 use Azure RBAC for access management. New Purview accounts created after that date require collection-based access management via Purview Governance Portal. Legacy IAM roles alone are insufficient for portal access on newer permission models.
solution: Configure roles from Microsoft Purview data plane using collection access management. Use InPrivate browser and have the Resource Group Owner grant Purview Root Collection Admin via REST API: POST /subscriptions/{subId}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Purview/accounts/{account}/addRootCollectionAdmin?api-version=2021-07-01 with body {objectId: userObjectId}. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/purview/catalog-permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Opening Microsoft Purview Governance Portal/Unable to access Purview Governance Portal through the Portal or URL

symptom: Only Collection Admin has left the organization. Remaining users cannot access Purview Governance Portal or assign roles to other people. Error: You do not have access to this Purview account. Contact an Owner or Collection Administrator for access. InvalidAuthenticationTokenAudience error may also appear.
rootCause: In Microsoft Purview, Collection Admin role is required to assign roles to others. When the sole Collection Admin leaves the organization, no one else can manage access through the portal.
solution: Use Service Principal (SPN) with REST API to add new Root Collection Admin: 1) Create SPN in AAD App registrations, get client ID/tenant ID/secret. 2) Assign SPN as Owner of Resource Group + Contributor of Purview Account via IAM. 3) Generate Bearer token via POST to https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/oauth2/token with grant_type=client_credentials, resource=https://management.core.windows.net. 4) Call POST https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subId}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Purview/accounts/{account}/addRootCollectionAdmin?api-version=2021-07-01 with Authorization: Bearer {token} and body {objectId: newAdminObjectId}.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Opening Microsoft Purview Governance Portal/Unable to access Purview Governance Portal through the Portal or URL

symptom: Purview Governance Portal shows Not Authorized error when Purview account is configured with Private Endpoints (VNET). Client machine is not on the same virtual network as the Purview Private Endpoint.
rootCause: When Purview is configured with Private Endpoints, only clients within the same VNET (or peered VNET) can access the Purview Governance Portal. Clients outside the configured VNET are denied access.
solution: Access the Purview Governance Portal from a client machine in the same virtual network (VNET) that has been configured for the Purview account Private Endpoint. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/purview/catalog-private-link-troubleshoot#common-errors-and-messages
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Opening Microsoft Purview Governance Portal/Unable to access Purview Governance Portal through the Portal or URL

symptom: Customer unable to see Data Factory or Data Share under External Connections in Purview Governance Portal. External Connections section is missing or empty despite having data factory resources.
rootCause: User does not have both Owner role at the Subscription level and Purview Data Curator role at the Purview Account level. Both roles are required to view and manage External Connections (Data Factory and Data Share).
solution: 1) Under Subscriptions, verify customer has Owner or Account Admin role. If not, assign Owner role. 2) Under Purview Account, add customer as Owner and Purview Data Curator. 3) Close any existing Purview Governance Portal and relaunch. Customer should now see External Connections (Data Factory and Data Share) and be able to add existing resources. Note: When connections are added, Purview automatically creates resources with Purview Data Curator role.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Roles and Permissions in Microsoft Purview/Roles to View External Connectors (ADF & OnPrem)

symptom: Failed to add or update asset in Purview Data Sharing; error when adding or modifying assets in a sent share
rootCause: (1) Storage container used as a target for receiving shared data cannot simultaneously be used to share data. (2) Exceeding sharing limits: a source storage account supports up to 20 targets, and a target storage account supports up to 100 sources.
solution: Do not reuse a receiving container as a sharing source. If sharing limits are exceeded, contact Microsoft Support to request a limit increase. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/purview/how-to-share-data

rootCause: User does not have Owner permissions at the storage account level.

symptom: Collection deletion fails with HTTP 409 error: The collection is being referenced by other resources and cannot be deleted, even after all visible assets in Data Map UI have been removed and no active scans are shown.
rootCause: Orphan sub-scan entities (sub-scans and sub-datasources created alongside the main scan prior to the October 2022 fix) are not deleted when the main scan is deleted. These invisible orphan entities maintain a reference to the collection in the Artifact Store, blocking deletion even though no assets are visible via UI or REST API.
solution: 1. Confirm no visible assets/datasources/child collections remain. 2. Verify user has Collection Admin role. 3. Use REST API (atlas/v2/search/advanced) to check for hidden references. 4. Query RP/ProjectBabylonLogEvent logs with correlation ID to locate orphan entities. 5. Confirm no Data Share references or blocking policies exist. 6. If all checks pass, raise IcM/AVA to Data Scan PG team to delete orphan entities from the Artifact Store.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset being referenced by other resources and can't be deleted Error 409

symptom: Unexpected assets appear at root collection level despite no scan configured at root. Assets reappear after customer deletes them. Customer may also receive 409 error when attempting to delete the root-level or lineage-linked child collection.
rootCause: ADF copy activities report asset lineage to the root collection level by design. Assets created via ADF pipelines are registered in the root collection as lineage data, not from a manual scan. Collections with scans created prior to October 2022 may also have orphan sub-scan entities blocking deletion.
solution: Inform customer that ADF lineage reporting to root collection is by design. Identify ADF copy activities as the source using Purview monitoring feature. For 409 deletion errors on lineage-linked collections, raise IcM/AVA to Data Scan PG. Direct customer to submit feature request at https://feedback.azure.com/d365community/forum/82d7bddb-fb24-ec11-b6e6-000d3a4f07b8
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset being referenced by other resources and can't be deleted Error 409

symptom: Azure Data Share snapshot fails with error: Failed because Blob is missing or cannot be located. Please check the blob name and path and retry later -- even though the Data Share service principal has Blob Data Reader role correctly assigned on the source storage account.
rootCause: A conditional access expression was incorrectly added to the RBAC role assignment for the Data Share service principal on the storage account, blocking all OAuth token requests despite the correct role (Blob Data Reader) being assigned.
solution: Review RBAC role assignments on the source storage account for the Data Share service principal. Identify and correct or remove the blocking conditional access expression. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/conditions-role-assignments-portal
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Share/Cannot view, create or edit share/Issue receiving data from Data Share sender

symptom: User cannot view Purview Data Share invitation despite receiving email notification
rootCause: Email invitation sent to alias instead of Azure login email; share already accepted (no longer shows in invites tab); guest user has not verified email in tenant; user lacks Read role in Purview collection
solution: Ensure data provider sends invitation to Azure login email (not alias); check Received Shares tab if share was already accepted; guest user must verify tenant email address (valid 12 months); assign Read role in relevant Purview collection
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Share/Purview Share/Issues with share invitation/Cannot view share invite

symptom: Failed to attach or receive a share in Microsoft Purview Data Sharing
rootCause: Insufficient permissions on target data store; container specified was created outside Purview Data Sharing; source and target storage accounts are identical; exceeded sharing limits (20 targets per source account, 100 sources per target account)
solution: Verify permissions per how-to-receive-share prerequisites; only map data to containers created during Purview asset mapping; use different source and target storage accounts; contact Support to request limit increase
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Share/Purview Share/Issues with share invitation/Failed to attach a share

symptom: Scan test connection fails for Azure Dedicated SQL Pool; Error 3801/3881: Failed to access the Azure Dedicated SQL pool with the Managed Identity
rootCause: Purview Managed Identity (MSI) missing Reader role in SQL Server IAM; SQL Server Networking not configured to allow Azure services access; database user for Purview MSI not created in the dedicated SQL database
solution: (1) Add Purview MSI as Reader in SQL Server Access Control (IAM); (2) Enable Allow Azure services access in SQL Server Networking exceptions section; (3) Run: CREATE USER [PurviewManagedIdentity] FROM EXTERNAL PROVIDER; (4) Run: EXEC sp_addrolemember db_datareader, [PurviewManagedIdentity]
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Sources/Azure Dedicated SQL Pool - Scan Test Connection Failed

symptom: Purview collection deletion fails with HTTP 409: The collection is being referenced by assets and cannot be deleted, even though the collection appears empty in the UX
rootCause: Zero-size files or temporary Spark files exist in the collection but are hidden by UX optimization that filters them from search results; these hidden assets still block collection deletion
solution: Option 1 (REST API): POST /api/search/query with collectionId filter to find all entity GUIDs, then call DELETE /api/atlas/v2/entity/bulk to delete them; repeat until no results returned. Option 2 (UX): Add feature flag &feature.ext.catalog={"enableToggleEmptyFile":"true"} to URL to reveal hidden zero-size files, delete them via toggle, then delete the collection
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/UX/Collection can’t be deleted because there’s referenced asset

symptom: Collection cannot be deleted with error 409: 'The collection is being referenced by assets and can't be deleted' even though collection appears empty in UX. Hidden assets (zero-size files, temp spark files) are not shown by default in the catalog UI.
rootCause: UX optimization hides zero-size and temporary files from search results. These hidden assets still reference the collection, preventing deletion.
solution: Option 1 (REST API): Use Search API (POST /api/search/query) with collectionId filter to find all entity GUIDs, then use bulk delete API (DELETE /api/atlas/v2/entity/bulk?guid=...) to remove them in batches. Repeat until no results. Option 2 (UX): Add feature flag '&feature.ext.catalog={"enableToggleEmptyFile":"true"}' to portal URL, toggle 'Hide zero-size file' off, delete visible assets, then delete collection.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/UX/Collection can't be deleted because there's referenced asset

symptom: Purview Portal is totally inaccessible (ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED) when accessing via Private Endpoint in VNET. Browser shows 'This site can't be reached' for web.purview.azure.com.
rootCause: DNS records for portal private endpoint are not correctly configured. The domains web.purview.azure.com, catalog.prod.ext.web.purview.azure.com, and web.privatelink.purviewstudio.azure.com must all resolve to the portal PE IP address.
solution: 1) Verify if Portal PE is truly needed - if VNET has public access, remove Portal PE (portal is static, no customer data transmitted). 2) If PE required, configure DNS per docs (Azure Private DNS Zone or custom DNS). 3) Verify with nslookup: 'nslookup web.purview.azure.com', 'nslookup catalog.prod.ext.web.purview.azure.com', 'nslookup web.privatelink.purviewstudio.azure.com' - all should resolve to portal PE IP. 4) Alternative: change Purview Network Config to 'Disabled Public for Ingestion only'.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/UX/Portal can't be opened via Private Endpoint

symptom: Purview Portal partially loaded with 'Initialization failed' and 'AxiosError: Network Error' when using Private Endpoint. The Microsoft Purview header/chrome loads but content initialization fails.
rootCause: Account Private Endpoint is not properly configured or account service is failing. Each Purview account needs its own separate Account PE (unlike Portal PE which is shared globally). Browser console shows failed API requests to {accountname}.purview.azure.com.
solution: Open browser console (Network tab), check failed API requests: If URL contains {accountname}.purview.azure.com -> Account PE or account service issue (verify Account PE is configured for this specific account). If URL contains web.purview.azure.com or xxx.prod.ext.web.purview.azure.com -> Portal PE or portal web app issue. Collect HAR file and escalate to engineering if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/UX/Portal can't be opened via Private Endpoint

symptom: ERR_CERT_COMMON_NAME_INVALID error in browser console when opening Purview Portal via Private Endpoint. The error affects API calls to the account endpoint ({accountname}.purview.azure.com).
rootCause: Purview account endpoint DNS is configured to point to the Portal PE IP instead of the Account PE IP. This causes a TLS certificate mismatch because the certificate served by the Portal PE does not match the account hostname.
solution: Verify DNS configuration: run 'nslookup {accountname}.purview.azure.com' and confirm it resolves to the Account PE IP (not Portal PE IP). Fix the DNS record to point the account endpoint to the correct Account PE IP address.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/UX/Portal can't be opened via Private Endpoint

symptom: User gets You need permission to access this error when navigating to Roles and permissions in Data Catalog after upgrading classic Purview account to Unified Purview Portal.
rootCause: When upgrading from classic Azure Purview to Unified Portal, only the original Purview Account creator AAD identity is placed in the Data Governance Admin role.
solution: Use self-service option to add users to Data Governance Role. Alternatively, identify the Purview Account creator via Azure Portal > Purview account > Properties > Created By. If unavailable, create ICM/AVA to Purview Data Catalog queue.

symptom: Customer observes unexpected charges under Data Management Basic DGPU despite not running any Purview scans.
rootCause: DGPUs are automatically spun for Data Estate Health (DEH) for ongoing monitoring of data governance in Purview Unified Catalog.
solution: Follow Data Health Billing Awareness documentation to disable DEH operations and stop automatic DGPU charges.

symptom: Customer wants to move a data product from one governance domain to another in Purview Data Governance.
rootCause: Moving data products between domains is not a supported feature.
solution: Delete the data product and recreate it in the target domain. Moving between domains is not supported.

symptom: Adding data assets to a data product fails with status 400: The name contains invalid character(s).
rootCause: Table schema contains forbidden characters (line feed, tab, whitespace-only, non-printable ASCII control characters).
solution: Edit the table schema in Data Map to remove forbidden characters from column/table names.

rootCause: A previously profiled column was renamed/deleted but DQ scan settings still reference it.
solution: Remove the non-existent column from DQ scan settings and re-run the scan.
